title: profile.yml
description: Structured user-layer ground truth. Loaded by jobify.profile_loader.load_profile() as a dict. WS-E should validate a generated profile.yml against this; WS-A2/WS-D/WS-F may rely on it. Only `identity` and `application_defaults` are hard-required — the rest are strongly recommended for scoring/tailoring quality but the loaders degrade gracefully when absent.

identity.description: Who the user is + contact info the submit pre-fill types into forms.

application_defaults.description: Canonical form-fill answers. EVERY key here is read by jobify/submit/adapters/_common.py::applicant_fields — do not rename. This is the single source of truth for these PII facts (load_application_defaults()).

visa_sponsorship_needed.description: true if sponsorship is needed now or in future.

previous_interview_with_company.description: Map of company-slug -> bool. May be {} initially; extend per company.

location_and_compensation.description: Hard logistics feeding the scorer's constraint checks. Recommended.

target_comp_usd.description: Range or single, as a string.

what_he_is_looking_for.description: Targeting by tier. Each tier is an object with at least a `label`; optional `notes`, `reference_role`. Recommended.

archetypes.description: Named lanes the tailor frames the candidate as (load_archetypes()). 2-4 generic lanes recommended. The classifier prompt reads keys + framings at runtime, so adding an entry makes it live immediately.

label.description: One-line human name for the lane.

framing.description: How to position the candidate for this lane.

emphasis_proof_points.description: Experiences to lead with for this lane.

key_technical_skills.description: Flat list of real, defensible skills. Recommended.
